content.full_text:
February 27, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: "Single Source" of a Gift
Request for Confirmation of Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter is to confirm your oral advice rendered on December 13, 1990 regarding interpretation of the term "single source" of a gift, as used in Government Code sections 89501, 89504, and 89505—the newly-enacted annual gift limits. Subsequent to our conversation in December, Mr. John Wallace has also given telephone advice on this issue.

Both you and Mr. Wallace advised that the "in fact directs and controls" test of Regulation 18531.5(c) and the "completely independently" test of 18531.5(d) were the standards to be applied in determining "single source" under the new law. These are the same tests which applied in construing the term "single source" in former Government Code section 85400, now repealed and replaced by the new sections cited above.

At your earliest convenience, I would appreciate confirmation that my understanding of the oral advice rendered by you and Mr. Wallace is correct.

Thank you for your courtesy.

Very truly yours,

JUDITH SPROUL DAVIS

JSD:kah
6353.01

cc: Dan Terry
     Kirk A. Pessner
